commented_code:
report_type: test_results
         token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
 
+  Discover-test-modules:
+    runs-on: blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404
+    outputs:
+      integration: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.integration }}
+      smoke: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.smoke }}
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          sparse-checkout: |
+            t/integration
+            t/smoke/tests
+      - id: scan
+        run: |
+          nested=$(find t/integration -mindepth 2 -name 'test_*.py')
+          if [ -n "$nested" ]; then
+            echo "::error::Integration test files must live at t/integration/ root, not in subdirectories: $nested"

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -89,55 +89,51 @@ jobs:
         report_type: test_results
         token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
 
+  Discover-test-modules:
+    runs-on: blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404
+    outputs:
+      integration: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.integration }}
+      smoke: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.smoke }}
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          sparse-checkout: |
+            t/integration
+            t/smoke/tests
+      - id: scan
+        run: |
+          nested=$(find t/integration -mindepth 2 -name 'test_*.py')
+          if [ -n "$nested" ]; then
+            echo "::error::Integration test files must live at t/integration/ root, not in subdirectories: $nested"
discussion_comments.comment_body: This should help avoid cases where someone adds integration tests with their own conftest, potentially overriding the expected setup. By forcing flat integration tests, we force-block any other conftest.py, forcing modification of the existing one, making breaking changes much clearer during review + breaking CI (blocking merge).

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -89,55 +89,51 @@ jobs:
         report_type: test_results
         token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
 
+  Discover-test-modules:
+    runs-on: blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404
+    outputs:
+      integration: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.integration }}
+      smoke: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.smoke }}
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          sparse-checkout: |
+            t/integration
+            t/smoke/tests
+      - id: scan
+        run: |
+          nested=$(find t/integration -mindepth 2 -name 'test_*.py')
+          if [ -n "$nested" ]; then
+            echo "::error::Integration test files must live at t/integration/ root, not in subdirectories: $nested"
discussion_comments.comment_body: The smoke tests encourage nested setups in some cases, so the limitation is only applied to the integration tests ([e.g](https://github.com/celery/celery/blob/main/t/smoke/tests/stamping/test_hybrid_cluster.py) same test running Celery 4 and Celery 5 workers in the same cluster)

discussion_comments.comment_body:
I see this is a debt from `main`, but it should be 3.10, 3.13 and 3.14.
We can’t skip 3.13, since we don’t officially support 3.14 yet, so it’s better to add it.

discussion_comments.commented_code:

+        default: '["3.10", "3.14"]'
discussion_comments.comment_body: I'm a bit confused about this config. At first I thought this is a range but turned out it's an array. Why don't we test all Python versions we support?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ on:
         description: 'JSON array of Python versions to test'
         required: false
         type: string
-        default: '["3.9", "3.14"]'
+        default: '["3.10", "3.14"]'
discussion_comments.comment_body:
> I'm a bit confused about this config. At first I thought this is a range but turned out it's an array. Why don't we test all Python versions we support?

Due to stability issues with our tests and lack of maintainability capacity, so we pick the min/max+3.14 in this case to trade off additional CI runs for easier maintainability on PRs (better rate at all CI passing without flaky issues).

discussion_comments.comment_body:
> Due to instability with the CI. This is a technical debt we need to solve.

@auvipy I prefer to handle it myself as I mentioned, because one of the reasons we did it was due to overly flaky state, requiring many re-runs on PRs etc., hurting our operational performance. Just reverting it will not be enough unfortunately. I want to fix the root cause of the flakyness and then enable it back so every CI run will consistently pass with rare cases of flakiness.
